zh.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde是Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde和Maria Mechtildis van Lier的兒子，於大約1162年出生在比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德。他於大約1182年與Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世於1220年享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde是Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde和Maria Mechtildis van Lier的儿子，于大约1162年出生在比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德。他于大约1182年与Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世于1220年享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde was born about 1162, in Oudenaarde, East Flanders, Belgium as the son of Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde and Maria Mechtildis van Lier. He married Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai about 1182. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died in 1220, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde est né environ 1162, à Audenarde, Flandre-Orientale, Belgique, fils de Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde et Maria Mechtildis van Lier. Il a épousé Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai environ 1182. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé en 1220, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde wurde um 1162 in Oudenaarde, Ostflandern, Belgien, als Sohn von Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde und Maria Mechtildis van Lier geboren. Er hat um 1182 Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb 1220 im Alter von 59 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde è nato il circa 1162 nel Oudenaarde, Fiandra Orientale, Belgio come figlio di Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde e Maria Mechtildis van Lier. Lui sposato Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai circa 1182. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì nel 1220, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaardeは約1162年にベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンオウデナアルドでHeer Arnoud III van OudenaardeとMaria Mechtildis van Lierの息子として生まれた。約1182年にRichilde de Mortagne de Tournaiと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼は1220年に59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde(은)는 대략 1162년 벨기에 스트-블라아더 우데나아르드에서 Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde(와)과 Maria Mechtildis van Lier의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1182년 Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1220년 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde nasceu aproximadamente 1162, em Oudenaarde, Flandres Oriental, Bélgica filho de Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde e Maria Mechtildis van Lier. Ele casou-se com Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai aproximadamente 1182. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1220, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde родился около 1162 г. в местности оуденаард, уст-влаандирен, Бельгия. Его родителей звали Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde и Maria Mechtildis van Lier. Он женился на женщине по имени Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai около 1182 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер в 1220 г. в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Gilbert II van Oudenaarde nació aproximadamente 1162, en Oudenaarde, Flandes Oriental, Bélgica como hijo de Heer Arnoud III van Oudenaarde y Maria Mechtildis van Lier. Se casó con Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai aproximadamente 1182. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió en 1220, a la edad de 59 años.
